coaching.coaching: Pin down acceptance criteria completeness and verifiability with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: acceptance criteria completeness and verifiability

coaching.coaching: Plan how active_triggers population from surviving findings will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: active_triggers population from surviving findings

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: 4 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In the `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` ownership bullet (or a linked corpus table), explicitly map PORT-F1–F4 to the four counterexample dimensions and define at least two distinct must-not-fire categories for PORT-N3 and PORT-N4 beyond 'no portability discussion' and 'specialization behind project config'.
evidence: Plan names 'PORT-F1 through PORT-F4' and 'PORT-N1 through PORT-N4' without defining their individual content
Compatibility counterexample matrix lists four dimensions but never says PORT-F1 = harness, PORT-F2 = target project, etc.
Only two must-not-fire categories are described in the plan body; four named negative cases are implied by PORT-N1–N4
findings.finding: PORT-F1 through PORT-F4 and PORT-N1 through PORT-N4 are referenced in two ACs as pre-defined named cases, but the plan never specifies what each individual case covers. The counterexample matrix supplies four dimensions (harness, target project, platform, project location) that likely map to PORT-F1–F4, but the mapping is not stated. More concretely, only two must-not-fire categories are described ('no portability discussion' and 'specialization behind project config'), yet four negative cases (PORT-N1–N4) are implied, leaving an executor of indigoid-unmystic-serpent to independently invent the missing two.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `indigoid-unmystic-serpent proves the corpus contains exactly PORT-F1 through PORT-F4...` and `indigoid-unmystic-serpent proves a plan with no portability discussion is a must-not-fire case`
scenarios: An executor implementing indigoid-unmystic-serpent constructs PORT-N3 and PORT-N4 from scratch, producing cases that do not match whatever Pass-4 reviewers expected, causing the balanced-corpus AC to be contested at review time.
Two independently assigned executors build incompatible corpora because the case-to-dimension mapping was never pinned.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short corpus table to the Child delivery map section, e.g.: PORT-F1 = harness assumption (Claude Code-specific CLI call), PORT-F2 = target-project assumption (Python-only lint rule), PORT-F3 = platform/venue assumption (macOS-specific dependency), PORT-F4 = project-location assumption (assumes CWD is checkout root); PORT-N1 = plan silent on portability, PORT-N2 = behavior behind project config, PORT-N3 = behavior behind explicit extension boundary, PORT-N4 = plan references supported platform but only as an example without encoding it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `indigoid-unmystic-serpent`'s prompt edits require re-running or updating `immune-floury-toad`'s prompt-content assertions (e.g., state that kakarikis's `make test` gate supersedes toad's AC for prompt fidelity, or that serpent owns a follow-up update of those assertions as part of its delivery).
evidence: AC: 'proves the resolved rubric contains the finding threshold plus the exact silence and project-configuration non-finding rules. Verify: …-k "prompt_resolution or finding_threshold or omission_guard or project_configuration_guard"'
Child delivery map: 'On a threshold miss, it may reword finder instructions or contrast examples only in `.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project-portability.md`'
No AC or delivery-map clause prescribes re-validating toad's prompt-content assertions after serpent's changes.
findings.finding: `immune-floury-toad`'s AC verifies exact prompt content (silence rule, project-configuration guard, finding threshold) via `test_project_portability_criterion.py`, but `indigoid-unmystic-serpent` is explicitly permitted to modify the same file (`.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project-portability.md`) after toad's delivery. No reconciliation mechanism is stated: if serpent rewrites the prompt, toad's 'exact content' unit tests become retroactively broken, yet re-running toad's AC suite is not prescribed in serpent's or kakarikis's delivery.
findings.location: AC line 2 (immune-floury-toad prompt_resolution/omission_guard/project_configuration_guard) vs Child delivery map (indigoid-unmystic-serpent: 'may reword finder instructions or contrast examples only in `.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project-portability.md`')
scenarios: Serpent rewrites a finder instruction to improve recall; toad's `omission_guard` or `project_configuration_guard` assertion now fails because the exact phrasing changed; the failure is only caught at kakarikis's `make test`, leaving toad's AC in an unresolved state.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the serpent delivery-map entry: 'If serpent edits the prompt, it also updates `test_project_portability_criterion.py` assertions for prompt content so that toad's AC suite remains green; the final verification of prompt correctness transfers to the `make test` gate in kakarikis.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact file path and format of the `taurine-catchable-kakarikis` documentation artifact (e.g., `docs/extending-rebar.md` or an ADR at `docs/adr/NNN-project-extension-pattern.md`) so discoverability is testable and the AC is unambiguous.
evidence: 'taurine-catchable-kakarikis — owns the client-facing dogfood documentation, parser-safe placement outside the built-in move table, and final lint/typecheck/test verification.'

AC reads: 'taurine-catchable-kakarikis documents the exact project criterion and project move composition outside the built-in move table. Verify: .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_docs.py'
No file path (e.g., docs/extending-rebar.md, an ADR file, or a README section) is named anywhere in the plan for this artifact.
The plan introduces 'project extension seams' as a new architectural concept for client authors — a non-trivial addition that warrants a findable doc artifact.
findings.finding: The plan promises documentation of the new project-extension pattern but never specifies where the documentation artifact lives (file path, format, or section). The AC only verifies via `test_project_portability_docs.py`, which can assert content but does not guarantee the doc is discoverable by client authors following the extension seam.
findings.location: Child delivery map — `taurine-catchable-kakarikis` / Success Criteria / AC: `taurine-catchable-kakarikis` documents the exact project criterion and project move composition
scenarios: A client author wants to add their own project criterion and cannot locate the reference documentation because its path is unspecified.
A future plan reviewer cannot verify the doc was created at the right location because the test only checks content, not path/discoverability.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the `taurine-catchable-kakarikis` section and the corresponding AC: the doc artifact is created at `docs/extending-rebar.md` (or equivalent), and the pytest verify asserts both the file exists at that path and contains the criterion/move composition example.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or `CLAUDE.md` update (owned by `taurine-catchable-kakarikis`) capturing the architectural decision: why the project extension boundary sits in `criteria_routing.json` + `plan_review_moves.json` rather than in core, what the extension contract guarantees, and what remains intentionally outside project-scope control.
evidence: 'taurine-catchable-kakarikis — owns the client-facing dogfood documentation, parser-safe placement outside the built-in move table, and final lint/typecheck/test verification'
'This approach uses the existing project extension seams instead of changing the generic four-pass kernel. It directly demonstrates the intended boundary'
No mention of ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or internal architectural decision record anywhere in the plan

The extension pattern (two config files composing into the four-pass kernel) is described as reusable by client authors but the design rationale and contract boundaries are not captured in a durable internal artifact
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel two-seam project extension architecture (project criterion overlay via `criteria_routing.json` + project move via `plan_review_moves.json`) as the canonical pattern for future rebar clients, but plans only client-facing dogfood documentation — no ADR or CLAUDE.md update is planned to record the architectural decision (why the extension boundary sits here, what the contract is, and what is intentionally NOT extensible at project scope).
findings.location: Scope / Child delivery map — `taurine-catchable-kakarikis` deliverable; 'Approach and sequencing' section
scenarios: A future rebar contributor wants to extend the criterion system in a new direction and cannot determine from documentation alone whether the extension should go in `criteria_routing.json`, in the core kernel, or in a new file — the ADR would answer this.
A client author reads the dogfood docs but needs to know what the extension contract guarantees vs. what might change — that stability contract is implicit only.
findings.suggested_fix: In `taurine-catchable-kakarikis`'s scope, add: 'Authors an ADR (e.g., `docs/adr/0NNN-project-extension-seam.md`) or `CLAUDE.md` section recording the design rationale for the two-seam extension architecture, the stability contract for project overlays, and the explicit boundary between project configuration and reusable core behavior.' Add a corresponding AC: `pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_docs.py -k adr_or_design_doc_present`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml` explicitly to indigoid-unmystic-serpent's scope and file-impact list, and add an AC that the eval fixture is present and well-formed (loadable by `calibrate_criterion`) before the live eval run is attempted.
evidence: indigoid-unmystic-serpent scope: 'owns the balanced live eval corpus, four-part positive findings, must-not-fire boundaries, and repeat-run thresholds'

AC: '[operator-attested] indigoid-unmystic-serpent records three live eval runs over PORT-F1–PORT-F4 plus PORT-N1–PORT-N4... Verify: .venv/bin/rebar criteria eval project.portability --runs 3 --output json'
eval.py:57-60: eval_spec_path returns .rebar/evals/plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml; if absent and no packaged spec, raises EvalError
Scope says indigoid-unmystic-serpent 'owns the balanced live eval corpus' but names no fixture file path
findings.finding: The indigoid-unmystic-serpent child's scope omits the eval fixture file that the live eval command requires. calibrate_criterion() resolves the fixture via criterion_prompt_id('project.portability') → 'plan-review-project-portability' → .rebar/evals/plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml (eval.py:57-60). Without this file, the operator-attested AC ('rebar criteria eval project.portability --runs 3 --output json') raises EvalError before any LLM call, making the live eval unrunnable.
findings.location: Child delivery map / indigoid-unmystic-serpent scope; Acceptance Criteria (operator-attested live eval AC)
scenarios: Implementer authors PORT-F1–PORT-F4 / PORT-N1–PORT-N4 as unit-test fixtures only (inside test_project_portability_eval.py), but never writes .rebar/evals/plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml; the live eval command raises 'no eval spec for prompt plan-review-project-portability' before any LLM call.
A second implementer working on taurine-catchable-kakarikis runs 'make test' which passes (offline tests pass), but the operator-attested AC cannot be closed because the eval fixture is missing.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to indigoid-unmystic-serpent's scope: '.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml (the eval fixture the calibrate_criterion/rebar criteria eval command loads; must contain PORT-F1–PORT-F4 as must-fire cases and PORT-N1–PORT-N4 as must-not-fire cases in the .eval.yaml dataset format)'. Add an offline AC: 'The eval fixture is present and passes validate_eval_spec. Verify: pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_eval.py -k eval_fixture_valid'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit `depends_on` link from child f698 (taurine-catchable-kakarikis) to child 3863 (immune-floury-toad) in the ticket's formal deps field, matching the dependency stated in f698's plan text.
evidence: f698 deps field lists only: [{target_id: '2ad8-f56e-7bc2-4948', relation: 'depends_on'}, {target_id: '21dd-8681-4447-4bc0', relation: 'depends_on'}]
f698 plan text says: 'Depends on the criterion, coaching, and calibration children so the documentation describes the final validated behavior rather than an intermediate design.'
f698 Approach documents criterion routing, prompt path, rubric headings, matrix values, and non-finding rules — all authored in child 3863, not in 2ad8 or 21dd
Child 21dd transitively depends on 3863, so the transitive path f698→21dd→3863 exists, but a runner using only direct deps would not enforce it
findings.finding: Child f698 (taurine-catchable-kakarikis) declares only two formal deps (2ad8 and 21dd) but its plan text states it 'depends on the criterion, coaching, and calibration children' — omitting an explicit dep link on child 3863 (immune-floury-toad). The docs subsection documents content that originates exclusively from 3863: the routing entry with all five parameter values, the prompt path and six front-matter fields, the four rubric headings with typed field markers, all four client-shape matrix label-value pairs, and both exact non-finding rules. An execution runner that honours only the formal dep graph could schedule f698 to start while 3863 is still in progress, allowing the documentation child to describe design-time intent rather than the final authored artefacts.
findings.location: child f698-b895-f6e8-4044 — deps field and Approach/Dependencies section
scenarios: An automated scheduler that reads only formal dep links starts f698 concurrently with 3863; the documentation test asserts literals from a prompt file that has not yet been authored, causing the test to fail or to validate against a placeholder
A future re-ordering of the eval corpus (21dd) does not trigger a re-check of the doc child's routing/prompt literals, which come from 3863 not 21dd
findings.suggested_fix: Add `{target_id: '3863-a783-b38f-4c8a', relation: 'depends_on'}` to f698's `deps` field so the formal dependency graph matches the stated three-child dependency described in f698's plan and enforces correct scheduling order.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (in `truthful-specified-silverfish` or `immune-floury-toad`) that structurally verifies the end-to-end linkage: a synthetic finding with `criteria: ['project.portability']` populates `active_triggers` and causes the `project-portability` move to appear in `applicable_moves()`, closing the gap between the rubric prose instruction and the applicability-filter schema that depends on it.
evidence: Plan: 'applies_when: ["project.portability"]' on the project-portability move in `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json`
coach.py:855 — `triggers = {c for f in blocking + surviving for c in f.get('criteria', []) or []}` — active_triggers built from finding criteria fields
coach.py:106 — `move_applies`: checks `set(aw) & set(active_triggers)` — requires exact string match of 'project.portability'
immune-floury-toad ticket Approach: 'criteria: list[str] containing project.portability' is a PROSE INSTRUCTION in the rubric body, not a schema constraint
No AC in truthful-specified-silverfish or immune-floury-toad verifies the structural path: rubric-emitted finding.criteria contains 'project.portability' → active_triggers → move applies
The existing output schema (plan_review_findings) does not constrain the vocabulary of values in the criteria[] field
findings.finding: The `project-portability` Pass-4 move's applicability depends on `applies_when: ['project.portability']` matching the `active_triggers` set, which is built from `f.get('criteria', [])` on surviving findings. The plan specifies this linkage only as a rubric prose instruction ('criteria: list[str] containing project.portability' under 'Required finding fields'), but no schema enforcement, validator, or test is specified to confirm that the LLM-generated finding actually populates `criteria` with the exact string `'project.portability'`. If the LLM omits or misspells this value, the move silently never becomes applicable — the processing rule is not co-located with the applicability-filter schema that depends on it, and there is no AC that structurally verifies the end-to-end `criteria → active_triggers → applies_when` linkage.
findings.location: What section — `project-portability` move spec with `applies_when: ["project.portability"]`; `immune-floury-toad` Approach — 'Required finding fields' rubric section; `truthful-specified-silverfish` AC — 'applies only when project.portability survives review'
scenarios: The LLM emits a `project.portability` finding with `criteria: ['portability']` or `criteria: []` (omitting the namespace prefix). The `applies_when` match fails silently; the `project-portability` move is never offered; coaching is absent with no error or test catching it.

A future rubric edit removes or rephrases the 'Required finding fields' section. The applicability linkage breaks invisibly because no structural test validates the end-to-end `criteria field value → move applicability` chain.
findings.suggested_fix: In `truthful-specified-silverfish`, add a test that calls `coach.applicable_moves(registry, active_triggers={'project.portability'})` and asserts the `project-portability` move is returned, plus a complementary assertion that `applicable_moves(registry, active_triggers=set())` does NOT return it. This directly validates the processing rule co-located with the schema (move registry + applicability filter) rather than relying solely on rubric prose.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
